mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal fails to separate winners from losers with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and weakening economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions during tightening cycles do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns, or the predictive accuracy drops below 45% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while core inflation declines, it confirms a successful disinflationary policy shift that preserves real yields and corporate margins, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The divergence between rising FEDFUNDS and falling CPIAUCSL yields a negative or zero 5-day forward return in > 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate structural labor market weakness that reduces aggregate consumption and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS declines remains below 0.03, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative during payroll contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% signals robust economic expansion that validates corporate earnings forecasts and attracts institutional capital, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising unemployment during a liquidity crunch indicates severe supply-demand mismatch, prompting rapid equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The combined signal of declining INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a win rate above 55% during identified liquidity stress periods.
